# Hestia Foundation — Build Protocol
# Shell A (37.5m²) — Step-by-Step Construction Manual

**Version:** 1.0  
**Date:** 2026-05-29  
**Location:** Lagos, Algarve, Portugal  
**Build team:** 5–10 volunteers, no specialist skills required  
**Build duration:** 5–10 days weathertight, 10–15 days complete  
**License:** CC BY-SA 4.0

---

## Pre-Build Checklist

### Site Preparation (Before Day 1)
- [ ] Site cleared and levelled (200m² minimum)
- [ ] Soil test completed (for earth screw suitability)
- [ ] Water access arranged (temporary connection or tank)
- [ ] Electrical access arranged (temporary connection or generator)
- [ ] Base camp established (container, tent, or vehicle for tool storage)
- [ ] Material delivery scheduled (flatbed trailer, crane not required)
- [ ] All materials on-site and inspected
- [ ] Tools inventory checked
- [ ] Safety briefing completed for all volunteers
- [ ] Weather forecast checked (avoid rain days for panel assembly)

### Material Inventory

#### Structural (€8,000)
| Item | Quantity | Unit | Notes |
|------|----------|------|-------|
| EPAL Euro pallets (1200×800mm, heat-treated, ISPM-15) | 35 | each | Verify HT stamp |
| Timber I-joists (38×90mm, 3.6m span, CE-marked) | 120 | lm | Engineered timber |
| OSB sheathing (12mm, 1220×2440mm, structural grade) | 35 | sheets | EN 300, OSB/3 |
| Construction adhesive (polyurethane, 600ml tubes) | 20 | tubes | For panel bonding |
| Ring-shank nails (3.1×90mm, galvanised) | 10 | kg | Structural connections |
| Wood screws (4.0×60mm, TX20, galvanised) | 5 | kg | OSB face fastening |
| L-brackets (galvanised steel, 50×50×35mm) | 40 | each | Panel-to-panel connection |
| Timber joists (47×150mm, treated, 3.6m) | 12 | each | Floor and roof structure |

#### Foundation (€2,500)
| Item | Quantity | Unit | Notes |
|------|----------|------|-------|
| Earth screws (galvanised steel, 76mm diameter, 1.5m length) | 16 | each | Load-rated 25kN |
| Timber posts (100×100mm, treated, 2.4m) | 16 | each | Corner and intermediate posts |
| Timber beams (47×200mm, treated, 5.0m) | 6 | each | Perimeter beams |
| Adjustable post bases (galvanised) | 16 | each | For levelling |

#### Envelope (€3,000)
| Item | Quantity | Unit | Notes |
|------|----------|------|-------|
| Windows (double-glazed, uPVC or timber, 1200×1200mm) | 4 | each | U-value ≤ 1.4 |
| Door (insulated, 900×2100mm) | 1 | each | With threshold |
| Roof membrane (EPDM or bituminous, 4m²) | 50 | m² | Including overlaps |
| Rain gutter (aluminium, 5m sections) | 4 | sections | With brackets |
| Downpipe (aluminium, 2m sections) | 2 | sections | With bends |

#### Insulation (€1,500)
| Item | Quantity | Unit | Notes |
|------|----------|------|-------|
| Rockwool batts (100mm, 1200×600mm, 35kg/m³) | 60 | m² | Wall cavities |
| Rockwool batts (150mm, 1200×600mm, 35kg/m³) | 40 | m² | Roof |
| Vapour barrier (polyethylene, 1000 gauge) | 60 | m² | Warm side |
| Breather membrane (UV-resistant) | 60 | m² | Cold side |

#### Off-Grid Systems (€5,000)
| Item | Quantity | Unit | Notes |
|------|----------|------|-------|
| Solar PV panels (455W, monocrystalline) | 8 | each | 3.64kWp total |
| Inverter/charger (3.6kW, hybrid) | 1 | each | With MPPT |
| Battery (10kWh, lithium, wall-mounted) | 1 | each | 48V system |
| Solar hot water panel (2m², flat plate) | 1 | each | With 200L tank |
| Composting toilet (separating, ventilated) | 1 | each | With 12V fan |
| LED lighting (12V, 5W, IP44) | 8 | each | Interior and exterior |

#### Interior (€2,000)
| Item | Quantity | Unit | Notes |
|------|----------|------|-------|
| Engineered oak flooring (14mm, 190mm wide) | 40 | m² | Click-lock |
| Walnut worktop (40mm, 600mm wide) | 4 | lm | Kitchen surface |
| Pine tar (natural wood preservative, 5L) | 2 | tins | Exterior timber |
| Bronze hardware (hinges, handles, hooks) | 1 | set | Door and window |
| Paint (mineral, breathable, white, 10L) | 2 | tins | Interior walls |

---

## Tools Required

### Power Tools
- [ ] Cordless drill/driver (18V, 2 batteries) × 3
- [ ] Circular saw (185mm blade) × 2
- [ ] Jigsaw × 1
- [ ] Reciprocating saw × 1
- [ ] Angle grinder (for earth screw installation) × 1
- [ ] Impact driver × 2
- [ ] Laser level × 1
- [ ] Compressor + nail gun (optional) × 1

### Hand Tools
- [ ] Hammers (claw, 450g) × 4
- [ ] Handsaws (550mm) × 2
- [ ] Chisels (set of 4) × 1
- [ ] Screwdrivers (set) × 2
- [ ] Tape measures (5m) × 4
- [ ] Spirit levels (600mm, 1200mm) × 2 each
- [ ] Carpenter's square × 2
- [ ] Clamps (various sizes, 20 total) × 20
- [ ] Pliers, wrenches, adjustable spanners × 1 set

### Safety
- [ ] Hard hats × 10
- [ ] Safety glasses × 10
- [ ] Work gloves × 10 pairs
- [ ] Ear defenders × 5
- [ ] Dust masks (FFP2) × 20
- [ ] Steel-toe boots × 10 pairs
- [ ] First aid kit (comprehensive) × 1
- [ ] Fire extinguisher (powder, 2kg) × 2

---

## Day-by-Day Build Protocol

### DAY 0 — Arrival and Setup
**Morning (08:00–12:00)**
- [ ] Team arrival and accommodation check
- [ ] Site walk-through and safety briefing
- [ ] Tool inventory and setup
- [ ] Material inspection and organisation
- [ ] Layout marking (string lines, stakes)

**Afternoon (13:00–17:00)**
- [ ] Confirm earth screw locations (grid: 1.5m × 1.5m)
- [ ] Mark building footprint (7.5m × 5m)
- [ ] Pre-cut timber to length (if not pre-cut by supplier)
- [ ] Prepare panel fabrication area (flat, covered, 6m × 4m)
- [ ] Set up cutting station with dust extraction
- [ ] Photograph all materials for documentation

**End of Day 0 Checklist:**
- [ ] All materials accounted for
- [ ] Tools operational
- [ ] Site marked and level reference established
- [ ] Team roles assigned (2 fabricators, 2 installers, 1 general, 1 documenter)
- [ ] Photo: Site before build

---

### DAY 1 — Foundation and Floor Structure
**Morning (08:00–12:00)**
- [ ] Install earth screws using angle grinder with socket adapter
  - 16 screws at 1.5m centres
  - Depth: minimum 1.2m into ground
  - Torque check: all screws resist 25kN vertical load
- [ ] Cut timber posts to height (accounting for slope, max variation 50mm)
- [ ] Install adjustable post bases on screw heads
- [ ] Erect posts, brace temporarily

**Afternoon (13:00–17:00)**
- [ ] Install perimeter beams (47×200mm) on post bases
  - Check level with laser level (max 10mm variation across entire footprint)
- [ ] Install intermediate floor joists (47×150mm) at 400mm centres
  - Double joists at panel junctions
- [ ] Fix joists with joist hangers or direct nailing
- [ ] Lay 12mm OSB floor decking
  - Stagger joints, glue and screw
  - Leave 3mm expansion gap at perimeter

**End of Day 1 Checklist:**
- [ ] Foundation level and square (diagonals equal ±10mm)
- [ ] Floor structure complete
- [ ] Floor load test: 2 people jumping simultaneously, no bounce >5mm
- [ ] Photo: Foundation and floor complete

---

### DAY 2 — Wall Panel Fabrication (Part 1)
**Morning (08:00–12:00)**
- [ ] Set up panel fabrication jig on flat surface
  - Use two 3.6m I-joists as parallel rails
  - Spacing: 1.2m (internal panel width)
- [ ] Fabricate first panel (1.2m × 3.6m):
  1. Lay bottom rail (I-joist offcut, 1.2m)
  2. Place 3 vertical I-joists at 400mm centres
  3. Apply construction adhesive to all joints
  4. Nail with ring-shank nails (3 per joint)
  5. Check square (diagonals equal ±5mm)
  6. Staple temporary braces to maintain square during curing

**Afternoon (13:00–17:00)**
- [ ] Fabricate 3 more panels (4 total by end of day)
- [ ] For each panel:
  - Install pallet slat infill between I-joists (cut to fit)
  - Glue and nail slats
  - Check panel is flat (no twist >5mm)
- [ ] Stack panels flat, protected from sun/rain
- [ ] Label panels: W1, W2, W3, W4 (wall panels)

**End of Day 2 Checklist:**
- [ ] 4 wall panels fabricated
- [ ] All panels square and flat
- [ ] Adhesive curing (minimum 4 hours before handling)
- [ ] Photo: Panel fabrication process

---

### DAY 3 — Wall Panel Fabrication (Part 2) and Erection
**Morning (08:00–12:00)**
- [ ] Fabricate remaining wall panels (typically 4–6 more)
- [ ] Fabricate corner panels (L-shaped, with 600mm return)
- [ ] Install OSB sheathing on one face of all panels:
  - Apply adhesive bead to I-joist flanges
  - Lay OSB sheet, stagger joints
  - Screw at 150mm centres to all joists and rails
  - Check flush (no bumps >2mm)

**Afternoon (13:00–17:00)**
- [ ] Erect first wall panel:
  - 4 people minimum (panel weight: 45kg)
  - Align with floor edge, plumb with spirit level
  - Temporarily brace with 2×4 timber diagonals
  - Fix base to floor joists with L-brackets
- [ ] Erect second panel adjacent:
  - Align panel edge, check plumb
  - Connect with L-brackets at top, middle, bottom
  - Check corner is square (90° ±1°)
- [ ] Continue erection until all walls standing

**End of Day 3 Checklist:**
- [ ] All wall panels erected and braced
- [ ] Walls plumb (±5mm per 3m)
- [ ] Walls square (corner angles 90° ±1°)
- [ ] Temporary bracing secure against wind
- [ ] Photo: Walls erected

---

### DAY 4 — Wall Completion and Window/Door Openings
**Morning (08:00–12:00)**
- [ ] Install OSB sheathing on exterior face of all panels
  - Glue and screw, same method as interior
  - Cut openings for windows and door with jigsaw
  - Save cutouts for possible reuse
- [ ] Install vapour barrier on interior face
  - Staple to joists, overlap seams 100mm, tape
- [ ] Install breather membrane on exterior face
  - Staple, overlap 150mm, tape seams

**Afternoon (13:00–17:00)**
- [ ] Install window frames in openings
  - Check square and level
  - Fix through OSB into I-joist with screws
  - Seal perimeter with expanding foam
- [ ] Install door frame in opening
  - Check plumb and level
  - Fix and seal same as windows
- [ ] Install rockwool insulation in wall cavities
  - Cut to fit tightly, no gaps
  - Wear dust mask and gloves

**End of Day 4 Checklist:**
- [ ] Walls fully sheathed and insulated
- [ ] Windows and door installed and sealed
- [ ] No gaps in insulation visible
- [ ] Photo: Walls with openings

---

### DAY 5 — Roof Structure
**Morning (08:00–12:00)**
- [ ] Install top wall plate (47×150mm timber) around perimeter
  - Level and fix to top of wall panels
  - This is the bearing surface for roof joists
- [ ] Cut and install roof I-joists
  - Span: 5.0m (building width)
  - Spacing: 400mm centres
  - Birdsmouth cuts at wall plate (if applicable)
  - Fix with metal joist hangers or direct nailing

**Afternoon (13:00–17:00)**
- [ ] Install roof OSB decking
  - Stagger joints, glue and screw
  - Thickness: 18mm (or 12mm with 100mm I-joists)
- [ ] Install roof membrane
  - Roll out EPDM or bituminous membrane
  - Overlap seams 150mm, glue or heat-weld
  - Install upstands at parapets (if flat roof) or overhangs (if pitched)
- [ ] Install gutters and downpipes

**End of Day 5 Checklist:**
- [ ] Roof structure complete and secure
- [ ] Roof membrane fully sealed (no visible gaps)
- [ ] Water-tightness test: hose spray for 10 minutes, no interior leaks
- [ ] Photo: Roof complete

---

### DAY 6 — Services and Interior (Part 1)
**Morning (08:00–12:00)**
- [ ] Install electrical conduit and boxes
  - Run conduit through wall cavities (before insulation complete)
  - Position boxes for switches, sockets, lights
  - Keep 12V system separate from 230V (if any)
- [ ] Install plumbing (if applicable)
  - Cold water supply line
  - Drainage (if not composting toilet)
  - Hot water line from solar panel
- [ ] Install solar hot water panel on roof
  - Angle: 30–35° (Algarve latitude)
  - Orientation: South ±15°
  - Fix to roof structure with brackets

**Afternoon (13:00–17:00)**
- [ ] Install solar PV panels on roof or ground mount
  - 8 panels × 455W = 3.64kWp
  - Same angle and orientation as hot water
  - Connect to inverter/charger
- [ ] Install battery and inverter inside building
  - Wall-mounted, ventilated area
  - Connect PV, battery, and distribution board
- [ ] Install LED lighting
  - 12V system, connect to battery
  - Test all circuits

**End of Day 6 Checklist:**
- [ ] All services installed
- [ ] Solar systems operational (check voltage output)
- [ ] Lighting working
- [ ] Photo: Services installation

---

### DAY 7 — Interior Finishing (Part 2)
**Morning (08:00–12:00)**
- [ ] Complete rockwool insulation in roof
  - 150mm batts between roof joists
  - Install vapour barrier below insulation
- [ ] Install interior OSB or plasterboard lining (if desired)
  - Paint with breathable mineral paint
- [ ] Install engineered oak flooring
  - Acclimatise boards for 48 hours before laying
  - Click-lock installation, no adhesive needed
  - Leave 10mm expansion gap at perimeter

**Afternoon (13:00–17:00)**
- [ ] Install kitchen worktop (walnut)
  - Cut to length, oil with food-safe oil
  - Install sink and tap (if applicable)
- [ ] Install shelving and storage
  - Use I-joist offcuts for shelving brackets
- [ ] Install door and window hardware
  - Hinges, handles, locks
  - Adjust for smooth operation

**End of Day 7 Checklist:**
- [ ] Interior complete and livable
- [ ] Flooring fully laid
- [ ] Kitchen functional
- [ ] Photo: Interior finished

---

### DAY 8 — Exterior Finishing and Testing
**Morning (08:00–12:00)**
- [ ] Apply pine tar to exterior timber
  - All exposed timber: posts, beams, panel edges
  - Two coats, allow drying between coats
- [ ] Install exterior trim around windows and door
  - Cover panel edges, seal with silicone
- [ ] Install shutters (optional, recommended for security)
- [ ] Paint or stain any additional exterior elements

**Afternoon (13:00–17:00)**
- [ ] Conduct air-tightness test (smoke pencil or blower door if available)
- [ ] Thermal imaging scan (if available) to check insulation continuity
- [ ] Structural inspection: check all connections, braces, fixings
- [ ] Clean site, remove waste, organise tool inventory

**End of Day 8 Checklist:**
- [ ] Exterior weatherproof and finished
- [ ] No visible gaps or unprotected timber
- [ ] Site clean and safe
- [ ] Photo: Exterior complete

---

### DAY 9 — Commissioning and Handover
**Morning (08:00–12:00)**
- [ ] Test all systems:
  - Solar PV: check output, battery charging
  - Solar hot water: check temperature rise
  - LED lighting: all circuits
  - Composting toilet: fan operation
  - Windows and door: operation, locking
- [ ] Install sensor network (if monitoring):
  - Temperature/humidity sensors (interior, exterior, wall cavity)
  - Energy meter (PV output, battery state)
  - Structural deflection monitoring (if applicable)
  - Configure data logger and public dashboard

**Afternoon (13:00–17:00)**
- [ ] Final documentation:
  - As-built drawings (measure and record actual dimensions)
  - Photograph every detail
  - Video walkthrough
  - Record any deviations from protocol
- [ ] Team debrief and lessons learned
- [ ] Celebrate completion

**End of Day 9 Checklist:**
- [ ] All systems operational
- [ ] Sensor network live and data logging
- [ ] Documentation complete
- [ ] Photo: Team celebration

---

### DAY 10 — Buffer and Contingency
**Full day reserved for:**
- [ ] Any unfinished items from Days 1–9
- [ ] Quality corrections
- [ ] Additional testing
- [ ] Early-stage monitoring data review
- [ ] Prepare for 12-month monitoring period

---

## Post-Build Monitoring Protocol

### Monthly (Months 1–12)
- [ ] Download sensor data
- [ ] Photograph exterior and interior
- [ ] Check for any structural movement or settlement
- [ ] Check exterior timber condition (pine tar integrity)
- [ ] Record any maintenance required

### Quarterly (Months 3, 6, 9, 12)
- [ ] Structural deflection measurement (laser level or dial gauge)
- [ ] Thermal comfort survey (occupant feedback)
- [ ] Energy performance review (PV output vs. consumption)
- [ ] Water usage review (if metered)

### Annual (Month 12)
- [ ] Comprehensive structural inspection
- [ ] Full thermal imaging survey
- [ ] Air-tightness re-test
- [ ] Publish annual performance report
- [ ] Publish updated build protocol with corrections

---

## Safety Protocols

### Mandatory
- Hard hats worn when overhead work in progress
- Safety glasses when cutting or drilling
- Dust masks when cutting OSB or insulation
- Gloves when handling timber or metal
- Steel-toe boots at all times on site

### Prohibited
- Working alone (minimum 2 people)
- Working at height without fall protection
- Using power tools while fatigued
- Working in rain or high wind (>Force 6)
- Consuming alcohol before or during work

### Emergency
- First aid kit located: ________________
- Nearest hospital: ________________
- Emergency contact: ________________
- Fire extinguisher locations: ________________

---

## Documentation Checklist

### Daily (During Build)
- [ ] Date and weather
- [ ] Team members present
- [ ] Activities completed
- [ ] Materials used
- [ ] Photos (minimum 20 per day)
- [ ] Video clips (minimum 5 per day)
- [ ] Any issues or deviations
- [ ] Lessons learned

### Final Deliverables
- [ ] Complete photo library (organised by day)
- [ ] Video edit (3–5 minute build montage)
- [ ] As-built drawings (PDF + CAD)
- [ ] Bill of materials with actual costs and supplier details
- [ ] Tool list with recommendations
- [ ] Safety incident log (hopefully empty)
- [ ] Team feedback survey
- [ ] 12-month monitoring dataset

---
